hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic and hydrophobic interactions at positions 5 and 6 with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues will improve selectivity margin by stabilizing SSTR2 binding while reducing off-target compatibility.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is effective for selectivity but needs refinement in structural and docking robustness. Tightening structural constraints and increasing candidate diversity will help achieve a higher-quality pass rate.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reduce clash tolerance to enforce better structural quality and reduce false positives due to steric clashes.
Increase the number of candidates to improve diversity and increase the chances of generating structurally valid and docking-passing designs.
